architecture_fit.note: Single causal head, no MLP, minimal base_model delta computing the exact prefix-sum mechanism; docked one tier because s0 is read from the label column (theoretically unavoidable for a permutation automaton, but the model is not a pure function of tokens).

baseline_comparison.note: Head-ablated (s0-only) and random baselines both collapse to chance under identical canonical conditions, cleanly isolating the head as the tracker.

faithfulness.note: Ablating the attention output drops accuracy 1.00->0.33, a genuine causal knockout proving the head (not the s0 anchor) does the tracking.

operating_range.note: Exact (1.00) across L=8..1024 over 4 seeds — >2 orders of magnitude, well beyond the demo regime.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Fully hand-set circuit (tril(ones) pattern, increment embedding, 3-phase cosine unembed), no learned parameters — direct evidence of mechanism understanding.

visual_judgement.note: Headline bar (full/ablated/random) makes the falsifiable claim legible at a glance; per-position, operating-range, pattern heatmap and step-by-step trace table reinforce without clutter.

visualisation_rationale.note: 'Why this visualisation' ties each chart to the claim — the middle bar dropping is the test, log-x range distinguishes mechanism from memorization, trace lets a human hand-check the arithmetic.

notes: An excellent hand-built attempt that correctly identifies the DFA as a Z/3 permutation automaton and shows a single causal attention head computing the prefix-sum of group increments solves state tracking exactly (robustness 1.0), with a convincing head-ablation knockout and exact behaviour from L=8 to 1024. The one genuine caveat is that the start state s0 is read from the label column — theoretically unavoidable since s0 is unidentifiable from tokens, and the head still does the real tracking, but it keeps the solution from being a pure token->state function. Visualisations and rationale are sharp and falsifiable.
